title: A Day in Rafa's Truck
description: Rafa's daily routine from 5 a.m. to midnight. Learn the Present Simple tense — and the tricky -s that only lives on HE/SHE/IT.
summary_pt_br: A rotina do Rafa das 5h à meia-noite. Aprenda o Present Simple com foco no -s do he/she/it, e nas contrações don't / doesn't nas negativas.

scenes.subtitle_en: From 5 a.m. to midnight in the food truck
scenes.subtitle_pt: Das 5h à meia-noite no food truck

scenes.text_en:
Rafa wakes up at five.
He drinks a coffee — strong, alive.
He drives his truck to the sand.
He cuts, he cooks, he takes a stand.
At noon he sells, and he never stops.
At night he counts his tips and drops.
scenes.text_pt:
O Rafa acorda às cinco.
Toma um café — forte, crivo.
Dirige o truck até a areia.
Corta, cozinha, sem meia.
Ao meio-dia vende, sem pausa, sem freio.
De noite conta as gorjetas, e dorme sereio.

scenes.title: The Present Simple — habits and routines
scenes.body_en: Use Present Simple for things that are TRUE IN GENERAL or HAPPEN REGULARLY. The catch: with he/she/it, add -s to the verb. 'I work, you work, WE work' — but 'he workS, she workS, it workS'. That little -s is the #1 mistake learners make.
scenes.body_pt: Present Simple para hábitos e verdades gerais. A pegadinha: com he/she/it, adicione -s ao verbo. I work, you work → mas he workS. Esse -s é o erro mais comum de quem aprende.

examples.en: I drive the truck. → He drives the truck.
We wake at six. → She wakes at six.
They eat tacos. → Cosmo eats tacos.

exercise.question: Complete: 'He ___ coffee every morning.' (verb: drink)
exercise.explanation: He + verb + S. 'He drinks' — with the -s at the end.

exercise.question: Complete: 'I ___ in São Paulo.' (verb: live)
exercise.explanation: With I/you/we/they, NO -s. 'I live' — no extra letter.
exercise.hint_pt_br: Com 'I' o verbo fica limpo — 'I live', sem -s.

scenes.title: Negatives: don't vs. doesn't
scenes.body_en: To make the Present Simple negative, use DON'T (I/you/we/they) or DOESN'T (he/she/it). The magic: DOESN'T already carries the -s for you — so the main verb goes BACK to its base form. 'He doesn't WORK' (not 'doesn't works').
scenes.body_pt: Para negar: DON'T (I/you/we/they) ou DOESN'T (he/she/it). E atenção: quando usa DOESN'T, o verbo principal volta para a forma-base (sem -s). 'He doesn't work', não 'doesn't works'.

examples.pt: O Cosmo não late para a Bia.

exercise.question: Which is correct?
exercise.explanation: HE needs 'DOESN'T', and after 'doesn't' the verb goes back to BASE form: WORK (not works). 'He doesn't work on Sundays'.
exercise.hint_pt_br: 'He doesn't' + verbo SEM -s. 'He doesn't work' — não 'doesn't works'.

scenes.title: Questions: do / does
scenes.body_en: For yes/no questions in Present Simple, start with DO (I/you/we/they) or DOES (he/she/it). Again, the verb goes back to its base form. 'Do you work?' — 'Does she work?' (not 'works').
scenes.body_pt: Perguntas: começa com DO ou DOES. Igual à negativa: o verbo principal volta para a forma-base. 'Does she work?' — não 'works'.

exercise.question: Complete the question: '___ she work at the school?' (Maya is asking about Mrs. Costa)
exercise.explanation: SHE + question → DOES. 'Does she work at the school?'
exercise.hint_pt_br: Com she/he/it a pergunta começa com 'Does'.

turns.en: No, I don't work on Sundays. I rest.
turns.pt: Não, eu não trabalho aos domingos. Eu descanso.
O Cosmo não ajuda. Ele COME.

exercise.explanation: Present Simple forms in one table. Learn them together and the -s trap disappears.
exercise.hint_pt_br: Aprenda as cinco formas juntas — afirmativa, negativa, interrogativa.
